children.text: in the case of a program operating on a Statewide basis, other than a program referred to in paragraph (3) or (4), $1,500,000;
in the case of a program operating on less than a Statewide basis, other than a program referred to in paragraph (3) or (4), $750,000;
in the case of a program operated wholly within one service area of the Bureau of Indian Affairs by an eligible entity referred to in , $450,000; orsection 4951(1)(D) of this title
in the case of a program operated wholly within more than one service area of the Bureau of Indian Affairs by an eligible entity referred to in , $1,500,000.section 4951(1)(D) of this title

children.heading: .—Determinations on Scope of Operations
children.label: b .—Determinations on Scope of Operations
children.text: A determination of whether a procurement technical assistance program is operating on a Statewide basis or on less than a Statewide basis or is operated wholly within one or more service areas of the Bureau of Indian Affairs by an eligible entity referred to in shall be made in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense.section 4951(1)(D) of this title

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: c .—Exception
children.text: The value of the assistance provided in accordance with is not subject to the limitations in subsection (a).section 4959(b) of this title

An eligible entity that earned income in a specified fiscal year from activities carried out pursuant to a procurement technical assistance program funded under this chapter may expend an amount of such income, not to exceed 25 percent of the cost of furnishing procurement technical assistance in such specified fiscal year, during the fiscal year following such specified fiscal year, to carry out a procurement technical assistance program funded under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: shall notify the Secretary of the amount of any income the eligible entity carried over from the previous fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: may retain an amount of such income equal to 10 percent of the value of assistance furnished by the Secretary under this section during the previous fiscal year.

In determining the value of assistance furnished by the Secretary under this section for any fiscal year, the Secretary shall account for the amount of any income the eligible entity carried over from the previous fiscal year.

children.heading: .—Funding From Other Federal Agencies
children.label: e .—Funding From Other Federal Agencies
children.text: The Secretary may accept and use funds from other Federal agencies and departments for execution and administration of the program authorized by this chapter.
